---
title: "AI4SoilHealth App"
---

The AI4SoilHealth App is the main user-facing digital environment around the toolbox. It helps users bring together field observations, uploaded information, contextual layers, and visual outputs.

![](../images/digital/app-architecture.png){.tool-photo width="85%"}

| Item | Description |
|---|---|
| **What it is** | A digital interface for interacting with soil-health information. |
| **Main purpose** | To connect local observations, measurements, and broader contextual information. |
| **Where used** | Digital |
| **Typical users** | Farmers, land managers, advisors, researchers, environmental professionals |
| **Typical outputs** | Maps, observations, records, comparisons, and report-ready summaries |

## What users can do with it

- view available layers and contextual information,
- store or explore local observations,
- connect measurements to locations,
- inspect maps and tables,
- and support communication through digital outputs.

## Relevant descriptor groups

The app can support all main descriptor groups by acting as the place where information is organised, explored, and presented.
